Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: A part of the physical world or a virtual world that is inherently both located in this world and having a 3D spatial extent. Concept from the BOT ontology.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: 区域是物理世界或虚拟世界的一部分，既位于现实空间中又可在3D空间中拓展。bot:Zone的子集包括bot:Site, bot:Building, bot:Storey, or bot:Space。bot:Zone实例可包含其他bot:Zone实例，从而可以对区域进行分组或细分。 同时，bot：Zone的实例也可以与其他bot：Zone实例相邻。最后，bot：Zone可以实例化为bot：Element的两个关系，它们可以包含在（bot：containsElement）中，或者与其相邻（bot：adjacentElement）。

Concept: Property
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#containsZone
Label: contains zone
Definition: Relationship to the subzones of a major zone. A space zone could for instance be contained in a storey zone which is further contained in a building zone.

Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)

Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#BuildingElement
Label: Building Element
Definition: The building element comprises all elements that are primarily part of the construction of a building, i.e., its structural and space separating system. Building elements are all physically existent and tangible things.

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#hasElement
Label: has element
Definition: Links a Zone to an Element that is either contained in, adjacent to or intersecting with the Zone. The intended use of this relationship is not to be stated explicitly, but to be inferred from its sub-properties. It will, for example, allow one to query for all the doors of a building given that they have an adjacency to spaces of the building.

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Property
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#has3DModel
Label: a modèle 3D
Definition: Liittää vyöhykkeen (bot:Zone) tai elementin (bot:Element) sen 3D-mallin yksilöivään IRI:in. Tämä 3D-malli voi olla kuvattu jollakin siihen tarkoitetulla RDF-sanastolla. 3D-mallin IRI voi myös osoittaa johonkin olemassa olevalla dataformaatilla kuvattuun 3D-malliin, joka on ladattavissa IRI:n kautta.

Concept: Class
Ontology: dstv.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://standards.buildingsmart.org/IFC/DEV/IFC4/ADD2_TC1/OWL#Material
Label: Material
Definition: Material is a homogeneous or inhomogeneous substance that can be used to form elements (physical products or their components). Material is the basic entity for material designation and definition; this includes identification by name and classification (via reference to an external classification). Material must have a name (=material grade e.g. S355JR) and a category (=material e.g. Steel, Aluminium etc.). Material defines the planned/proposed material. The finally used material is stored in the property set.

Concept: Class
Ontology: th_sharedvocalulary.owl (CORE)
URI: https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/thinkhome/ontology/BuildingOntologySharedVocabulary.owl#GeometricParameter
Label: GeometricParameter
Definition: Parameter describing the geometry of elements described in the building information model

Concept: Class
Ontology: datex_2016-06-13.n3 (CORE)
URI: http://vocab.datex.org/terms#Dimension
Label: Dimension
Definition: A component that provides dimension information. The product of width and height must not be necessarily be the square footage (e.g. in multi-storey buildings or when some zones are not part of the square footage).

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4agri_2020-06-05.n3 (CORE)

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4city_2020-06-05.n3 (EXTENSION)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Device
Label: Device
Definition: A tangible object designed to accomplish a particular task in households, common public buildings or offices. In order to accomplish this task, the device performs one or more functions. For example, a washing machine is designed to wash (task) and to accomplish this task it performs a start and stop function. Devices can be structured in categories (subclasses) that reflect the different domain in which a device is used, e.g., smart appliances domain (subclass FunctionRelated) vs. building domain (subclass BuildingRelated) vs. smart grid domain (subclass EnergyRelated). New categories can be defined,if needed, to reflect other differences, for example different points of view, such as the point of view of the device's user vs. the point of view of the device's manufacturer. We propose a list of devices that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

Label: has device characteristic

Concept: Property
Ontology: s4ener_2020-06-04.n3 (EXTENSION)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/saref4ener/nodeRemoteControllable
Label: node remote controllable
Definition: Indicates whether the power profile of a device is configured for remote control (e.g., by an energy management system).This refers to the selection chosen by the user on the remote control feature of the device. If nodeRemoteControllable is equal to FALSE, all the supported power sequences shall set the sequenceRemoteControllable property to FALSE. If nodeRemoteControllable is equal to TRUE, at least one power sequence shall be remotely controllable by setting the sequenceRemoteControllable property to TRUE.

Label: BuildingEnvironment

URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#Apartment
Label: Apartment
Definition: An apartment is a group of spaces, partial spaces or other zones.

Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: (1) a separately controlled heated or cooled space. (2) one occupied space or several occupied spaces with similar occupancy category, occupant density, zone air distribution effectiveness, and zone primary airflow per unit area. (3) space or group of spaces within a building for which the heating, cooling, or lighting requirements are sufficiently similar that desired conditions can be maintained throughout by a single controlling device.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: Zóna
Definition: 区域是物理世界或虚拟世界的一部分，既位于现实空间中又可在3D空间中拓展。bot:Zone的子集包括bot:Site, bot:Building, bot:Storey, or bot:Space。bot:Zone实例可包含其他bot:Zone实例，从而可以对区域进行分组或细分。 同时，bot：Zone的实例也可以与其他bot：Zone实例相邻。最后，bot：Zone可以实例化为bot：Element的两个关系，它们可以包含在（bot：containsElement）中，或者与其相邻（bot：adjacentElement）。

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Class
Ontology: s4watr_2020-06-03.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Device
Label: Device
Definition: A tangible object designed to accomplish a particular task in households, common public buildings or offices. In order to accomplish this task, the device performs one or more functions. For example, a washing machine is designed to wash (task) and to accomplish this task it performs a start and stop function. Devices can be structured in categories (subclasses) that reflect the different domain in which a device is used, e.g., smart appliances domain (subclass FunctionRelated) vs. building domain (subclass BuildingRelated) vs. smart grid domain (subclass EnergyRelated). New categories can be defined,if needed, to reflect other differences, for example different points of view, such as the point of view of the device's user vs. the point of view of the device's manufacturer. We propose a list of devices that are relevant for the purpose of SAREF, but this list can be extended.

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4agri_2020-06-05.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Function
Label: Function
Definition: The functionality necessary to accomplish the task for which a Device is designed. A device can be designed to perform more than one function. Functions can be structured in categories (subclasses) that reflect different points of view, for example, considering the specific application area for which a function can be used (e.g., light, temperature, motion, heat, power, etc.), or the capability that a function can support (e.g., receive, reply, notify, etc.), and so forth.

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Class
Ontology: saref_2020-05-29.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Switch
Label: Switch
Definition: A device of category saref:Actuator that performs an actuating function of type saref:OnOffFunction or saref:OpenCloseFunction

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4ener_2020-06-04.n3 (EXTENSION)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/saref4ener/ActuatorSwitch
Label: Actuator switch
Definition: Basic on/off operations on a simple actuator can be modelled with the ActuatorSwitch class. Whether the function turns a device itself ON or OFF, or whether it switches a specific feature, depends on the implementation. For example, one could model the super freeze program of a freezer using ActuatorSwitch class. An ON command would then activate the super freeze program and an OFF command would deactivate it. This example shall just give an idea how ActuatorSwitch can be used for more purposes than only turning devices on and off.

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4city_2020-06-05.n3 (EXTENSION)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Actuator
Label: Actuator
Definition: A device responsible for moving or controlling a mechanism or system

Concept: Class
Ontology: mep.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://pi.pauwel.be/voc/distributionelement#Actuator-ELECTRICACTUATOR
Label: Electric Actuator
Definition: a device that electrically actuates a control element

Concept: Class
Ontology: saref_2020-05-29.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/core/Actuator
Label: Actuator

building.ttl: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
Zones may contain other zones, Buildings may contain storeys, Storeys may contain spaces.
There are building elements, which may have sub elements.
A building space is a geographical point characterized by acertain altitude, latitude and longitude
bot.ttl: Zones are areas with spatial 3D volumes, and include, Buildings, Storeys, and Spaces.
Zones may intersect, or be adjacent to other zones.
Zones may have elements, either contained, adjacent, or intersecting it.
A adjacent zones and/or elements share some interface.
Zones and Elements have a 3D Model (including geometry, material, etc.).

**Veredicto Final**

Recomendación de Red:

1. **building.ttl**
2. **bot.ttl**
3. **s4agri_2020-06-05.n3**

Justificación breve por ontología:

* **building.ttl**: Esta ontología cubre conceptos fundamentales como zonas, edificios, pisos y espacios, lo que la hace una base sólida para entender el contexto de los dispositivos y su ubicación en un edificio.
* **bot.ttl**: Esta ontología agrega información sobre las relaciones entre zonas y elementos, lo que permite describir cómo los dispositivos se relacionan entre sí y con su entorno.
* **s4agri_2020-06-05.n3**: Esta ontología proporciona información detallada sobre los dispositivos y sus funciones, lo que la hace útil para describir el comportamiento de los dispositivos en diferentes contextos.

Observaciones de cobertura y posibles huecos:

* La recomendación de estas tres ontologías cubre una amplia gama de conceptos relacionados con edificios, dispositivos y su ubicación.
* Sin embargo, se puede observar que algunas ontologías como **beo.ttl**, **saref_2020-05-29.n3**, **s4inma_2019-04-30.n3**, **s4city_2020-06-05.n3** y **s4watr_2020-06-03.n3** no se han incluido en la recomendación, ya que no cubren conceptos nuevos o importantes que no estén ya abarcados por las tres ontologías recomendadas.
* La ontología **dogont_2015-01-11.n3** y **brick.ttl** tampoco se han incluido, ya que su cobertura es más específica y no agrega conceptos nuevos o importantes a la recomendación.

En general, esta recomendación de red de ontologías proporciona una buena cobertura de los conceptos relacionados con edificios, dispositivos y su ubicación, lo que puede ser útil para describir y analizar diferentes escenarios en el contexto de la web semántica.
